title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
During an interrogation, the detective informs Hilary Webber that Ina Brent was found strangled in a phone booth around 12:30 p.m. following a speeding incident. Professor Puddy, who had been working with Hilary, expresses shock at the bizarre nature of the death. The detective reveals that Ina had gone to Hilary's aunt's house the previous day to see her, but Hilary claims she was unaware of Ina's motives. Hilary explains that Ina was often indecisive and prone to worry, citing an instance where Ina accidentally sent an incomplete manuscript to Mr. Armand Levin.

The detective suggests the murder might be connected to an event on September 9th involving a man on Wilbraham Crescent. He notes that the phone booth where Ina was killed is located on that same street. Hilary denies any connection to the location, stating she has avoided Wilbraham Crescent since her previous involvement there, and becomes visibly distressed, pleading with the detective to find the truth.

In a separate narrative, Colin Lamb reports to Colonel Beck in London. Beck informs Colin that Mr. Rimsay, a building engineer living at 62 New Moon Street, is acting suspiciously after receiving a mysterious assignment from a company with no traceable history, which prompted a sudden trip to Romania. To investigate, Beck provides Colin with a new identity as Nigel Trunch, a botanist studying rare plants in the Balkan Peninsula. Beck also mentions other suspicious individuals, including a retired mathematics professor, Mr. MacNorton, who resigned for health reasons but remains energetic and has cut off contact with friends.
